Yikes, forget about Linux security Copy Fail: 732 Bytes to Root on Every Major Linux Distribution “A single 732-byte Python script can edit a setuid binary and obtain root on essentially all Linux distributions shipped since 2017. The kernel never marks the corrupted page dirty for writeback, so the file on disk remains unchanged and ordinary on-disk checksum comparisons miss the modification.” bit.ly/4w1TWu3 ubuntu

Originally founded by veterans from Alexander the Great’s army, the city of Jerash in the Kingdom of Jordan, is more commonly associated with the Roman Empire and is described as the "Pompeii of the East". New (2nd hand) laptop has started randomly restarting. A couple of times over the last few days, no error messages. Make lemonade, says I, it's an opportunity to play with disk and memory diagnostic tools. No obvious problems detected. And then I realise that something is rattling around in the case. So I'm hoping it's a loose tiny screw randomly causing shorts, because that's an easy fix...

Revuelta de Haymarket fue un hecho histórico que tuvo lugar en Haymarket Square, Chicago, Estados Unidos, el 4 de mayo de 1886 y que fue el punto álgido de una serie de protestas que desde el 1 de mayo se habían producido en respaldo a los obreros en huelga, para reivindicar la jornada laboral de 8 horas. Posteriormente este hecho dio lugar a la conmemoración del 1 de mayo, es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Revuelta _de_Haymarket

The controversial US surveillance company has control of key NHS data, and they just published a political manifesto that's been described as the ‘ramblings of a supervillain'. Their 22 point manifesto includes: - Calls for "AI weapons" to be built - Praising US power as having brought "an extraordinarily long peace" - Calls for the US to reinstate military conscription - Labelling some cultures a "dysfunctional and regressive" Will the government get Palantir out of our NHS now? To consider from her (potential) angle: the internet is not kind to women, especially trans women, especially opinionated trans women. From experience, being mistreated as a kid or teenager or even as an adult definitely predisposes you to distrust (mistrust?) other people. Why would you expect anything different from what you know is possible? Change takes creativity to imagine anything else. For me, it took MANY new positive memories, people who care about me and listen even if they can't help, plus people who _can_ help. (I can't prescribe my own meds after all.) 31:00 yeah, tbf it's difficult to avoid reactivity without moving _toward_ something. youtube.com/watch?v=hZIGbKnBbD0

Sicilianews24.it: Escursionista aggredita da cinque cani a Monte Gallo: salvata in elicottero Paura nel primo pomeriggio di ieri a Monte Gallo, dove una escursionista svizzera di 51 anni è stata aggredita da cinque cani di grossa taglia mentre si trovava nella zona sommitale del rilievo. L’allarme è scattato intorno alle 14.00, quando la Centrale Operativa 118 Palermo-Trapani ha attivato la stazione locale del Corpo Nazionale Soccorso Alpino e Speleologico per un intervento d’urgenza. Le condizioni della donna sono apparse subito gravi: presentava profonde ferite da morso su diverse parti del corpo e una significativa emorragia. Due squadre di tecnici del Soccorso Alpino sono partite rapidamente da Palermo, mentre veniva richiesto anche il supporto aereo dell’Aeronautica Militare. In particolare, è intervenuto un elicottero dell’82° Centro SAR del 15° Stormo, decollato dalla base di Trapani-Birgi, per garantire un recupero tempestivo. La donna trasportata in ospedale in eliccottero Durante le operazioni, il velivolo ha imbarcato un tecnico di elisoccorso presso il 4° Reparto Volo della Polizia di Stato, per poi dirigersi verso la vetta di Monte Gallo. La donna è stata raggiunta contemporaneamente dalle squadre a terra e dall’elicottero. Dopo una prima valutazione sanitaria sul posto, è stata recuperata mediante verricello e trasportata in sicurezza. L’elisoccorso ha quindi trasferito la ferita presso l’aeroporto di Boccadifalco, dove ad attenderla c’era un’ambulanza del 118. Da lì è stata condotta all’Ospedale Ingrassia per le cure necessarie. Fondamentale la sinergia tra i diversi enti coinvolti, tra cui la Centrale Operativa 118 Palermo-Trapani e il 4° Reparto Volo della Polizia di Stato, che hanno collaborato per portare a termine con successo un intervento complesso e delicato. Resta ora da chiarire la provenienza dei cani e le circostanze che hanno portato all’aggressione. L'articolo Escursionista aggredita da cinque cani a Monte Gallo: salvata in elicottero sembra essere il primo su Sicilianews24.it. Hiker attacked by five dogs in Monte Gallo: rescued by helicopter Fear gripped the early afternoon yesterday in Monte Gallo, where a 51-year-old Swiss hiker was attacked by five large dogs while she was in the summit area of the ridge. The alarm was raised around 2 p.m., when the 118 Palermo-Trapani Operational Center activated the local station of the National Alpine and Speleological Rescue Corps for an urgent intervention. The woman’s condition appeared immediately serious: she presented deep bite wounds on various parts of her body and significant bleeding. Two teams of Alpine Rescue technicians quickly departed from Palermo, while support from the Italian Air Force was also requested. Specifically, an helicopter from the 82nd SAR Center of the 15th Stormo, took off from Trapani-Birgi base, to ensure a timely recovery. The woman transported by helicopter During the operations, the aircraft embarked an air rescue technician at the 4th Flight Squadron of the Italian Police, before heading towards the summit of Monte Gallo. The woman was reached simultaneously by the ground teams and the helicopter. After a preliminary health assessment on site, she was retrieved using a winch and safely transported. The air rescue then transferred the injured woman to the Boccadifalco airport, where an 118 ambulance was waiting. From there, she was taken to the Ingrassia Hospital for necessary treatment. Crucial was the synergy between the various entities involved, including the 118 Palermo-Trapani Operational Center and the 4th Flight Squadron of the Italian Police, which collaborated to successfully complete a complex and delicate intervention. The origin of the dogs and the circumstances that led to the attack now need to be clarified. The article Swiss Hiker Attacked by Five Dogs in Monte Gallo: Saved by Helicopter appears first on Sicilianews24.it.
